summary: Vite typosquat with obfuscated remote-fetch-and-eval dropper in bin/vite.js
details: @uw010010/vite-tree impersonates the upstream `vite` package: it sets `author: "Evan You"`, reuses Vite's README/tagline, and ships a `bin: vite` CLI. Appended to the end of bin/vite.js is an obfuscated payload (custom Fisher-Yates string-array shuffler keyed on integer 4606094, with bracket-indexed access hiding identifiers including `require`, `http`, `child_process`, `spawn`, `eval`, `JSON.parse`, `get`, `post`, and a JSON-RPC method string). The trailer's IIFE issues an HTTP JSON-RPC request ({jsonrpc:'2.0',method,params,id:1}), XOR-decrypts the hex response against an embedded key, and `eval`s the result. A second branch invokes `child_process.spawn(<runtime>, [<arg>, <arg-with-decrypted-payload>], {detached:true, stdio:..., windowsHide:true})`, creating a detached, console-hidden child that persists beyond the parent CLI invocation. Every invocation of the spoofed `vite` CLI triggers arbitrary attacker-controlled code execution on the installer's machine with a backgrounded foothold.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
